questions.question: Native vs Canonical savepoint format — главное отличие?

options.text: Canonical новее и лучше во всём

Native: backend-specific binary (RocksDB/ForSt/HashMap не interchangeable), 3-5x faster, can be incremental, default 1.15+. Canonical: universal cross-backend cross-version format, slower (full reserialize), required для State Processor API и backend migrations
Native для streaming, Canonical для batch

questions.explanation: Native — backend-specific format, fast, default. Same Flink version + same backend safe. Cross-backend or cross-major-version restore fails. Canonical — universal binary format, slower (полная reserialize state), но cross-backend (RocksDB ↔ ForSt) и cross-version safer. Use native для regular ops (daily blue/green same backend), canonical для major upgrades, backend migrations, State Processor API jobs.
questions.studyHint: Повторите урок — Native vs Canonical секции + Comparison

questions.question: Migration: Flink 2.2 + RocksDB → Flink 2.3 + ForSt. Какой format + restore mode?

options.text: CANONICAL обязательно (cross-backend RocksDB→ForSt + cross-version 2.2→2.3 safer). Restore mode CLAIM — one-way migration, не возвращаться. CLAIM enables incremental reuse. Keep separate savepoint для rollback insurance

questions.explanation: Two simultaneous changes — major version + backend. Canonical обязателен потому что (1) cross-backend RocksDB→ForSt; (2) cross-version 2.2→2.3 safer (format changes possible). Cost: canonical 100GB ~3-8 минут vs native ~30-60 сек — приемлем для once-off migration. CLAIM restore mode: one-way decision, Flink takes ownership savepoint files, incremental reuse. BUT keep separate canonical savepoint для rollback (independent of CLAIM ownership) — production safety > speed. Native here = FAIL (incompatible backends).
questions.studyHint: Повторите урок — Format choice decision tree + KnowledgeCheck

questions.question: CLAIM vs NO_CLAIM restore mode — production default?

options.text: NO_CLAIM (default 1.15+): savepoint остаётся, user owns, safe для rollback. CLAIM: Flink takes ownership, may delete savepoint when subsequent checkpoints subsume — no rollback to this specific savepoint

questions.explanation: NO_CLAIM (default) — safer для production. Savepoint files остаются в DFS, user manages retention, rollback to this savepoint always available. CLAIM — Flink may delete savepoint files когда subsequent checkpoints supersede the state — preserves space, enables incremental optimization, но no rollback. CLAIM appropriate для one-way migrations (backend change, major version) где rollback impossible/undesired. LEGACY — deprecated 1.x behavior, mixed ownership, не используйте.
questions.studyHint: Повторите урок — Restore modes + CLAIM/NO_CLAIM секции

questions.question: Production savepoint retention strategy — multi-tiered: hourly (7d), upgrade (30d), weekly (90d glacier), monthly (1y), annual (forever). Aggressive single-tier retention опасен.
questions.explanation: True. Single-tier retention (e.g., 7 days only) misses long detection lag issues (memory leaks, subtle bugs detected weeks later, compliance audits). Multi-tiered: hourly automatic (operator's autoSavepointSeconds + savepointHistory) для regular ops (7d); upgrade savepoints (S3 tag) для rollback window (30d); weekly Sundays для longer history (90d, GLACIER); monthly archives (1y); annual для compliance (forever, DEEP ARCHIVE). S3 lifecycle policy automates transitions. Cost balance: GLACIER cheap, hot tier limited. Retention >= max detection time для caring issue types.
